description: Fixes identified during security, documentation, and operator review of the feat/server-oauth-credential-mode branch. Contains P0 bugs, P1 security issues, and P2/P3 documentation/operator tasks.
title: OAuth credential mode review fixes

description:
retrieve_auth_code() in database.py validates 'service_account_email' is non-empty, but the field is intentionally stored as empty string since SA provisioning was deferred to on_session_establishment().

This means every POST /api/auth/session/exchange call returns HTTP 400 'Invalid or expired auth code' — the Phase 1 CLI flow is completely broken in this branch.
notes:
Root cause: database.py:245 checks 'if not service_account_email or not expires_at:' but service_account_email is always '' per the design decision in this branch.

Fix: Remove the 'not service_account_email' guard from retrieve_auth_code(). The SA email field is intentionally blank — SA provisioning happens later in on_session_establishment(). The only required fields are user_email and expires_at.

Affected code:
- database.py:245 — remove service_account_email from the guard check
- Also verify save_auth_code still stores user_email correctly (it does)
title: Bug: retrieve_auth_code always returns None — Phase 1 CLI flow broken

description:
When Google issues a rotated refresh token, generate_oauth_token() in token_generator.py stores only the minimum scopes for the current command — not the full originally-consented scopes.

After rotation, the stored refresh_token_scopes in Firestore reflects only the last-called command's scopes (e.g., 'calendar'). Subsequent consent checks in credential_router.resolve() will 403 for all other scopes (gmail, spreadsheets, etc.), forcing users to re-login even though they already consented.
notes:
Root cause: token_generator.py:481:
  await self._db.set_refresh_token(user_email, new_encrypted, ' '.join(scopes))
where 'scopes' is the minimum scope list for that specific command.

Fix options:
1. Read the existing scopes from Firestore before writing, then union with the current scopes.
2. Never overwrite scopes during rotation — only update the encrypted_token field. The scopes were set at consent time and should not change.

Option 2 is simpler and more correct. Add a separate set_refresh_token_only() method (or update set_refresh_token to have scopes=None meaning 'do not update scopes'), OR call set_service_account_email-style merge that preserves the scopes field.

Simplest fix: In generate_oauth_token(), when storing the rotated token, read the existing record's scopes first and pass those instead of the command scopes:
  record = await self._db.get_refresh_token(user_email)
  await self._db.set_refresh_token(user_email, new_encrypted, ' '.join(record.scopes))

This preserves the original consented scopes across rotations.
title: Bug: refresh token rotation overwrites full consented scopes with command-minimum scopes

description:
credential_router.resolve() enforces the scope allowlist by calling settings.is_scope_allowed(), which checks against DELEGATION_SCOPES. But in oauth mode, SA commands have scopes like 'spreadsheets' and 'documents' that are not in DELEGATION_SCOPES.

This works silently today only because DELEGATION_SCOPES defaults to empty (allow all). If an operator sets DELEGATION_SCOPES to lock down DWD commands, they will unexpectedly also block OAuth SA commands for sheets, docs, etc.
notes:
Root cause: credential_router.py:183 — is_scope_allowed() reads delegation_scopes config.

The scope allowlist in OAuth mode should be the OAUTH_SCOPES setting, not DELEGATION_SCOPES.

Fix: In Settings, add is_oauth_scope_allowed(scope_url) that checks against oauth_scopes instead. Use this method in credential_router.resolve() when the provider is OAuthRefreshProvider (or when credential_mode != 'sa+dwd').

Simpler approach: since OAUTH_SCOPES is already validated and used to request consent at login, and the consent check (record.scopes) already enforces per-user limits, the allowlist check for OAuth providers may be redundant. Consider: 
- For SA provider: no scope check needed (SA scopes are internal)
- For DWD provider: check against delegation_scopes (existing behavior)
- For OAuth provider: check against oauth_scopes OR skip (consent check handles it)

The clearest fix is to pass the allowlist into the route entry at construction time (router already has the settings), and check the right allowlist per provider type.
title: Bug: scope allowlist uses delegation_scopes for OAuth commands — wrong config key

description:
POST /api/admin/sessions/revoke-all marks Firestore session tokens as revoked but does NOT call credential_router.on_logout(), so the user's Google OAuth refresh token remains valid.

An operator revoking all sessions to lock out a compromised account believes the user is locked out. In OAuth modes (sa+oauth, oauth), the user retains a live refresh token that ExtraSuite's server could still use.
notes:
The /api/auth/oauth/revoke endpoint is the correct way to revoke the OAuth token, but it requires the user to call it themselves (requires a valid session token). Admins have no direct way to revoke another user's OAuth token.

Options:
1. Add an admin endpoint: POST /api/admin/oauth/revoke?email=<email> (admin-only)
2. Have revoke_all_sessions optionally also revoke the OAuth token  
3. At minimum: document this clearly in .env.template and server/CLAUDE.md

Short-term fix (for this PR): Add a prominent note in .env.template and CLAUDE.md explaining the gap. Long-term: consider admin OAuth revocation endpoint.

Also update the revoke_all_sessions response to include a warning when credential_mode != 'sa+dwd':
  {'revoked_count': N, 'oauth_token_revoked': False, 'warning': 'OAuth refresh token not revoked. Use /api/auth/oauth/revoke or delete from Firestore manually.'}
title: Docs/ops: session revoke-all does not revoke OAuth refresh token — user not fully locked out

description:
The OAuth scope mapping for SA commands exists in two places:
- config.py:18-30 as _OAUTH_SA_SCOPE_NAMES (short names)
- credential_router.py:42-54 as _OAUTH_SA_COMMAND_SCOPES (full URLs)

When a new SA command is added, both must be updated or they drift.
notes:
Fix: Remove _OAUTH_SA_SCOPE_NAMES from config.py. The get_oauth_sa_scope_url() method in Settings is only called from tests — if not used in production code, remove it. Have credential_router.py be the single source of truth via _OAUTH_SA_COMMAND_SCOPES.

Check if config.get_oauth_sa_scope_url() is actually called anywhere in production code:
  grep -r 'get_oauth_sa_scope_url' server/src/

If only in tests, remove the method from Settings and update the tests to use credential_router._OAUTH_SA_COMMAND_SCOPES directly.
title: Chore: consolidate duplicate OAuth SA scope maps in config.py and credential_router.py

description: .env.template:46 contains '# DELEGATION_ENABLED=true' but this config key does not exist in Settings and never did in this version. It will confuse operators who try to set it.
notes:
Fix: Remove the '# DELEGATION_ENABLED=true' line and its surrounding comment from .env.template.

Also check if there are any references to DELEGATION_ENABLED in the codebase that should be cleaned up:
  grep -r 'DELEGATION_ENABLED' server/
title: Docs: remove stale DELEGATION_ENABLED from .env.template

description:
server/CLAUDE.md has two issues after this branch:
1. The 'Adding a new command — checklist' section references 'command_registry.resolve_credentials()' which no longer exists — it is now 'credential_router.resolve()'
2. The checklist doesn't mention updating _OAUTH_SA_COMMAND_SCOPES in credential_router.py when adding a new SA command in oauth mode
notes:
Fix 1: Update the resolve_credentials() reference to credential_router.resolve() in the auth flow description.

Fix 2: Add a step to the 'Adding a new command' checklist:
  'If the new command is an SA-class command and CREDENTIAL_MODE=oauth is supported, add the command type and its OAuth scope URL to _OAUTH_SA_COMMAND_SCOPES in credential_router.py'

Also update the Firestore Collections table to show the new fields in the 'users' collection:
- encrypted_refresh_token: AES-256-GCM encrypted OAuth refresh token (present when CREDENTIAL_MODE != sa+dwd)
- refresh_token_scopes: Space-separated scope URLs consented at login
- refresh_token_updated_at: When the refresh token was last updated
title: Docs: update server/CLAUDE.md — stale function reference and missing checklist step

description: There is no documentation on what happens when OAUTH_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY must be rotated (compliance, compromise). All stored refresh tokens become undecryptable and every user must re-authenticate simultaneously.
notes:
Add a note to .env.template and/or server/CLAUDE.md (or website docs) covering:

1. What happens during key rotation: all refresh tokens in Firestore are encrypted with the old key. The server will fail to decrypt them with the new key, returning OAuthTokenError → HTTP 500. Users will see 'OAuth credentials expired or revoked — run extrasuite auth login'.

2. Impact: All users must re-authenticate. This is expected behavior for key rotation.

3. Recommended procedure:
   a. Deploy new server with NEW_OAUTH_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY (double-key period)
   b. Write a migration script that reads each user document, decrypts with old key, re-encrypts with new key, and updates Firestore
   c. Once all tokens are migrated, switch to new key only
   
4. Emergency procedure (immediate rotation):
   a. Update Cloud Run secret to new key
   b. Redeploy server
   c. All users re-authenticate over next 30 days as their session tokens are used
   d. Monitor re-authentication rate in access_logs

Add a short note to .env.template near OAUTH_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY: 'WARNING: Rotating this key requires re-encrypting all refresh tokens in Firestore or all users must re-authenticate. See deployment docs.'
title: Docs: add key rotation runbook for OAUTH_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY

description:
website/docs/api/auth-spec.md and website/docs/security.md do not document:
- Three credential modes (sa+dwd, sa+oauth, oauth) and their tradeoffs
- POST /api/auth/oauth/revoke endpoint
- Loss of agent attribution in Drive version history when using CREDENTIAL_MODE=oauth
- Required OAUTH_SCOPES and OAUTH_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY configuration
notes:
Files to update:
1. website/docs/api/auth-spec.md:
   - Add section: Credential Modes
   - Document sa+dwd (default), sa+oauth, oauth with tradeoffs table
   - Add POST /api/auth/oauth/revoke to endpoint list
   - Add the new OAUTH_SCOPES and OAUTH_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY config vars

2. website/docs/security.md:
   - Add note: CREDENTIAL_MODE=oauth loses per-agent attribution in Drive version history
   - Add note: OAuth refresh tokens stored encrypted in Firestore; encryption key management
   - Add note: session revocation vs OAuth token revocation — they are separate

Tradeoffs table to include:
| Mode | Agent attribution | DWD needed | Refresh token stored |
|---|---|---|---|
| sa+dwd | Yes | Yes | No |
| sa+oauth | Yes | No | Yes (encrypted) |
| oauth | No | No | Yes (encrypted) |
title: Docs: update website docs for OAuth credential modes and new endpoints

description:
Tests:
1. Round-trip: build a Document with headings, paragraphs, bold/italic, links, list, simple table, footnote → serialize to markdown → deserialize → serialize again → assert identical markdown
2. Diff test: start from pulled markdown, edit (change heading text, add paragraph, change list item) → deserialize edited → reconcile against base → assert batchUpdate requests match expected
Use existing golden file infrastructure where possible.
title: Write round-trip and diff tests for markdown format

description:
Implement deserialization from markdown files to Document API object (no indices set — reindex_document handles those). Key behaviours:
- Parse with mistletoe using custom token extensions
- Reconstruct correct ParagraphElement types for each inline element
- Reconstruct InlineObjectElement for <x-img>, Person for <x-person>, etc.
- Synthesize list IDs deterministically from list type (same as _from_xml.py)
- Parse [^fn.id] footnotes and reconstruct footnote segments
- Parse header.md/footer.md with segment_id frontmatter
- Tables: parse HTML <table> or GFM pipe table
- Append synthetic trailing paragraph to each segment (same as XML path)
title: Implement _from_markdown.py (markdown → Document)

description: Integration test for the markdown workflow: (1) create a new Google Doc, (2) pull-md to get markdown, (3) edit the markdown (make a change), (4) push-md to write back, (5) pull-md again into a fresh folder, (6) compare markdowns — must be identical
title: End-to-end test: create doc, pull-md, edit, push-md, pull-md again, compare markdowns

description: Add mistletoe to extradoc/pyproject.toml. Define the custom token classes for passthrough entities: XImage, XPerson, XCommentRef, XSoftBreak, XPageBreak, XChip. These are mistletoe SpanTokens that match <x-*> HTML-like tags.
title: Add mistletoe dependency and define markdown token models

description:
Epic: Use Google Docs named ranges to annotate special markdown elements (code blocks, callouts, blockquotes) in the document. Named ranges survive content edits (indices auto-update) and give unambiguous semantic meaning beyond visual formatting heuristics.

Naming convention: extradoc:codeblock:python, extradoc:callout:warning, extradoc:blockquote

Design doc: see conversation history / PR description for full design.
title: Named ranges as semantic annotation layer in markdown serde

description:
Build named-range index (startIndex → type:subtype) from documentTab.namedRanges at serialization time.

When encountering a table StructuralElement:
- Check se.startIndex against extradoc:codeblock:<lang> → serialize as fenced code block
- Check against extradoc:callout:<type> → serialize as > [!TYPE]\n> content
- Check against extradoc:blockquote → serialize as > blockquote
- No match → serialize as normal GFM/HTML table

Heuristic fallback (for docs not created by extradoc): single-cell table + monospace font + grey bg → treat as plain code block.

Entry point: document_to_markdown() receives namedRanges from DocumentTab.
title: Pull side: detect code blocks and callouts via named ranges in _to_markdown.py

description:
Parse fenced code blocks (mistletoe CodeFence) → single-cell table:
- Background: #f3f3f3 grey
- No visible borders (transparent/0pt)
- Font: Courier New 10pt
- One paragraph per line

Parse callout syntax > [!TYPE] → single-cell table with type-specific background:
- warning: #fff3cd, info: #d1ecf1, danger: #f8d7da, tip: #d4edda

Parse block quotes > text → single-cell table with left-border accent, named range extradoc:blockquote.

Store the element type (code block lang, callout type) as metadata on the table structure so the annotation pass can create the correct named range name.
title: Push side: parse fenced code blocks and callouts in _from_markdown.py

description:
After reconcile+push applies body changes, run annotation pass:

1. Fetch updated document
2. Walk all single-cell tables matching code block or callout visual signature
3. Check if extradoc:* named range already covers that table's range
4. If not: emit createNamedRange with correct name (type + language hint)

Language hint storage: embed as first line of cell content using a convention (e.g., a zero-width character prefix or a comment paragraph that is stripped during pull-side serialization). Alternative: pass language hint through from _from_markdown.py via a side-channel (e.g., store in the document model's namedRanges field as a pending annotation with a sentinel index).

This closes the round-trip: push → named range created → subsequent pull correctly identifies code blocks.
title: Annotation pass: createNamedRange for code blocks and callouts after push

description:
Extend the reconciler to diff named ranges that use the extradoc:* namespace:

1. Collect extradoc:* named ranges from base and desired docs
2. Emit deleteNamedRange for ranges removed in desired
3. Emit createNamedRange for ranges added in desired — but indices are post-body-change, so this must be a separate batch or use deferred index resolution

Required for: code block language changes (delete old named range + create new), explicit callout type changes.

The annotation pass (extrasuite-hon.3) handles the simpler case of new code blocks. This task handles the reconciler-level diffs for existing ones.
title: Reconciler: emit createNamedRange / deleteNamedRange for extradoc:* annotations

description: Push side: parse backtick inline code to text run with Courier New 10pt. Pull side: detect monospace font runs and wrap in backticks. Also fix _escape_md() which currently strips backticks.
title: Inline code: parse backtick spans and serialize monospace text runs

description: Bug: relative links like [LICENSE](LICENSE) round-trip as [LICENSE](http://LICENSE). Investigate whether the prefix comes from _from_markdown.py, the Google Docs API normalizing on write, or _to_markdown.py. Fix to preserve relative URLs verbatim.
title: Fix relative URLs in markdown links (http:// prefix being added)

description: End-to-end golden file tests: create doc with fenced code blocks, callouts, inline code. pull-md, verify markdown, edit, push-md, pull-md again, diff must be empty. Also unit tests for each new element type.
title: Tests: round-trip markdown with code blocks, callouts, inline code

description: Add pull-md (serialize as markdown) and push-md (alias for push, auto-detects format) to 'extrasuite doc' subcommands. Steps: (1) add format param to DocsClient.pull(), (2) add cmd_doc_pull_md + cmd_doc_push_md handlers in doc.py, (3) register subparsers and dispatch in __init__.py
title: Add doc pull-md and push-md CLI commands

description: Add format='markdown' option to serde.serialize() and serde.deserialize(). Store chosen format in index.xml so diff/push auto-detect. Update DocsClient.pull() to accept --format=markdown flag. document.md replaces document.xml; styles.xml is dropped.
title: Wire markdown format into serde public API and client

description:
Implement serialization from Document API object to markdown files. Key behaviours:
- Paragraphs: NORMAL_TEXT→plain, HEADING_1-6→#-######, TITLE/SUBTITLE→special
- Inline: bold→**, italic→*, strikethrough→~~, underline→<u>, link→[](url)
- Lists: bullet→-, numbered→1., checkbox→- [ ]/- [x]
- Tables: GFM pipe table when all cells default style; HTML <table> when any cell has style/colspan/rowspan
- Passthrough inline: <x-img id=>, <x-person email= name=>, <x-br/>, <x-chip url= title=>
- Footnotes: [^fn.id] inline, definitions collected at end
- Page breaks: <x-pagebreak/>
- Horizontal rule: ---
- Headers/footers: separate header.md/footer.md with YAML frontmatter containing segment_id
title: Implement _to_markdown.py (Document → markdown)

description: Set status to in_progress
title: State change: status → in_progress

acceptance_criteria:
- _to_markdown.py converts Document → markdown with custom x-* tokens for passthrough entities
- _from_markdown.py converts markdown → Document (no indices; reindex_document handles those)
- HTML tables used when cells have non-default styles or colspan/rowspan
- GFM pipe tables used for simple tables
- Footnotes use actual Google Docs footnote ID as marker [^fn.id]
- Headers/footers serialized to header.md/footer.md with segment_id in YAML frontmatter
- Bold/italic/strikethrough/underline (<u>) and hyperlinks preserved
- List types: bullet→-, numbered→1., checkbox→- [ ]/- [x]
- Passthrough tokens: <x-img>, <x-person>, <x-comment-ref>, <x-br>, <x-chip>
- Round-trip test: markdown→Document→markdown is identical
- Diff test: edited markdown produces accurate batchUpdate requests
description: Add bidirectional markdown serialization/deserialization as an alternative format to XML in extradoc. This enables agents to work with Google Docs in a simpler, more readable format.
title: Implement markdown serde for extradoc
